---
title: Build A Browser App
description: Start with camera rPPG; add browser EEG if you need brain signals; use Web Bluetooth to connect a Muse-compatible headset.
---

Use this page when you need to choose the right path.

This is the site’s main chooser page:

- **New app**: scaffold first
- **Existing app**: follow the numbered tutorial
- **Mental model only**: read the integration overview

The recommended capability order stays the same:

1. **rPPG first** for most browser apps
2. **EEG second** when your product needs brain signals
3. **Web Bluetooth third** when you need a live Muse-compatible headset in Chrome or Edge

## How To Read This Site

| Doc type | What it is | When to use it |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Quickstart / scaffold** | Fastest command path to a working app | New app, evaluation, comparison baseline |
| **Existing-app tutorial** | Numbered integration walkthrough | You already have a browser app and want a reliable happy path |
| **Integration overview** | Short conceptual model and minimal snippet | You want package boundaries and architecture, not a full walkthrough |
| **Reference** | API and CLI details | You already know which surface you need |

Pick one primary path and follow it through. Do not make readers bounce through
Quickstart, tutorial, and overview before writing code.

## 1. Start With Camera-Based rPPG

This is the default browser path for most teams because it does not require a
headset.

| If you are... | Go here | Why |
| --- | --- |
| Starting a new app | [Quickstart](/quickstart) → `rppg-web-demo` | Fastest path to a known-good baseline |
| Integrating into an existing app | [Add Camera-Based rPPG To An Existing Browser App](/tutorials/rppg-existing-app) | Numbered install → camera → session → cleanup path |
| Learning the package model first | [rPPG In A Browser App](/guides/rppg-browser) | Short overview of `createRppgSession()` and app boundaries |

## 2. Add Browser EEG If You Need It

Use EEG when your product specifically needs brain-signal analysis.

| If you are... | Go here | Why |
| --- | --- |
| Starting a new app | [Quickstart](/quickstart) → `eeg-web-demo` | Proves the WASM runtime before hardware |
| Integrating into an existing app | [Add EEG To An Existing Browser App](/tutorials/eeg-existing-app) | Numbered install → init → verify WASM path |
| Learning the package model first | [EEG In A Browser App](/guides/eeg-browser) | Short overview of `initEegWasm()` and package boundaries |

## 3. Turn On Bluetooth For A Headset

This is not a separate primary workflow from EEG. It is the transport layer on
top of the EEG stack.

| If you are... | Go here | Why |
| --- | --- |
| Starting a new app | [Quickstart](/quickstart) → `eeg-web-ble-demo` | Known-good BLE reference app |
| Integrating into an existing app | [Stream Muse-Compatible EEG Over Web Bluetooth](/tutorials/eeg-ble-live-stream) | Numbered transport setup walkthrough |
| Learning the transport model first | [Web Bluetooth With Supported Devices](/guides/web-bluetooth) | Short overview of `BleTransport` and platform constraints |

If you have not wired the EEG WASM path yet, do **section 2** (or its
tutorial) before the BLE live-stream walkthrough.

## Need Package-Level Help?

See [Package Selection](/package-selection) for when to depend on each published package and paths to avoid.
